title: About My Business Account – CRA account help - Canada.ca
content:
## CRA account help – About My Business Account
My Business Account is an online portal in your CRA account that lets you interact with the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) on various business accounts. Program accounts include GST/HST, payroll, corporation income taxes, excise taxes, excise duties, and more.
To access GST/HST accounts that are administered by Revenu Québec, go to: Revenu Québec's website
- Who can use My Business Account
- How to access My Business Account
- Services in My Business Account
- Online mail for business

## Who can use My Business Account
Business owners (including partners, directors, and officers) can use My Business Account. Authorized representatives (including accountants and employees) can access these services through Represent a Client in their CRA account .

## How to access My Business Account
To access My Business Account for the first time, the CRA must have your Social Insurance Number (SIN) on file, and you must add your business number (BN) to your CRA account.
Note: To add your SIN to your BN online through your CRA account, your business must have a GST/HST account or a corporation income tax account. You must also have a processed GST/HST return or a processed corporate income tax return for that account, because you’ll be asked to enter information from that return.
If you do not have this information, go to Maintain your business for other ways to update your SIN on your BN.
- Sign in to your CRA account
- From the Welcome page, select + Add account
- Select Business account
- Depending on whether you have a business number (BN) or not, do one of the following: You already have a BN Select Add an existing business number (BN) Do not use a 10-digit Québec Enterprise Number (NEQ) or an Ontario Business Identification Number (BIN) You do not have a BN Select Business Registration Online After you receive your BN, sign in to your CRA account and follow these steps again. If you do not know what your BN is or are not sure if you already have one, check options to find an existing BN .
- You already have a BN Select Add an existing business number (BN) Do not use a 10-digit Québec Enterprise Number (NEQ) or an Ontario Business Identification Number (BIN)

### You already have a BN
Select Add an existing business number (BN)
Do not use a 10-digit Québec Enterprise Number (NEQ) or an Ontario Business Identification Number (BIN)
- You do not have a BN Select Business Registration Online

### You do not have a BN
Select Business Registration Online
After you receive your BN, sign in to your CRA account and follow these steps again.
If you do not know what your BN is or are not sure if you already have one, check options to find an existing BN .
- Confirm Terms and Conditions if applicable
- Enter your 9-digit business number and select Submit
- If your SIN is already linked to your BN: You’ll receive confirmation that your BN has been added to your CRA account. You can then go back to the Welcome page and select Business account to access My Business Account.
- If your SIN is not linked to your BN:
- Select the GST/HST or corporation income tax program account that has a return already filed and processed by the CRA.
- Enter the information requested from that return, then select Submit .
- Once the CRA confirms your information, your SIN will be added to your business account and your BN will be added to your CRA account.

## Services in My Business Account
After signing in to your CRA account, select your business from the Welcome page to access all the services available in My Business Account.
List of services available in My Business Account
For details about our hours of service and browser requirements, go to CRA account availability .

## Online mail for business
Most correspondence is only available online in My Business Account by default, except when a business has changed its delivery method to receive paper mail. Make sure you register for email notifications from the CRA to find out when you have new mail to view in My Business Account.
For more details, go to Online mail for business

### Backup multi-factor authentication option
The CRA has introduced a backup multi-factor authentication (MFA) option requirement for your CRA account. If you don’t already have one, you will be asked to add a backup MFA option the next time you sign in. Backup options include either a passcode grid or a third-party authenticator app. You will be able to skip this step.
A new Pillar Two “PT” program account has been added for filing returns related to the Global Minimum Tax.
Help with using My Business Account

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
